author: @smithsmarine4885
text: a pig is not such a crazy idea they are very heavy and therefore exert a huge turning force (torque) on a wheel that they are walking in due to gravity as they walk up the side of the wheel, causing it to turn. It is a well-known fact that an oxen will outperform a horse in a motive force type situation ie: plowing. They will almost sink to their knees in an attempt to move something that is holding them back. look up John Seymore and his books on self-sufficiency.

author: @oculusangelicus8978
text: Working dogs are incredible animals. They are the kind of creatures that so thoroughly enjoy doing things like that that I still stand amazed at them. From seeing malamutes and Huskies chomping at the bit to pull a sled laden with supplies for small communities in more remote areas than one might think impossible; to my very own Border Collie/Blue Heeler cross dog who was so smart that she took up herding my laying hens into their coop at night time with barely a nudge from me! These dogs are intelligent and need to be occupied with doing tasks because of this intelligence. And what is more they LOVE doing it, and nothing is more rewarding to them than hauling that sled to wherever it needs to go, and they get a meal and praise from their "master"/owner for doing a good job. Dogs are remarkable animals and they are called Man's Best friend for a very good reason. And their desire to work and be praised by their owner is ingrained in their DNA. As a youth, my Mother owned a Pure bred Cocker Spaniel who was NEVER used as a hunting dog, and it was ALWAYS a hard time keeping her from bodies of water, especially if there were ducks or geese in those waters, and one day, our pet Budgie escaped from his cage, and was so worked up that he couldn't settle down in one spot and we could not get him to relax so we could return him to his cage, well he flew into the patio door window pane, and kind of knocked himself silly, and before we could act, "PEACHES" the Cocker Spaniel had the stunned bird in her mouth and was walking back into the kitchen where the cage was, for the bird! The Bird came to while in Peaches' mouth and was squawking blue murder. Peaches dutifully let go of the bird when we put out our hand to take him from her and the bird, other than knocking himself silly and then covered with dog slobber, was perfectly unharmed. Peaches, a Cocker Spaniel, and a species that was specifically bred to retrieve birds that were  shot and had landed in the water, and they were well known for their "cotton mouth" not marring either the feathers nor the meat of the bird while retrieving the bird. It comes to them like it is second nature, but truly it is their first nature to be such, and it is through selective breeding that these animals have their traits. I would have loved to have seen and observed the "turnspit" dog, it's a shame that they are actually extinct, they would have made a good house pet and we all know dogs have incredible endurance for running or walking, they can do it for a long time, and would be happy to do so.

author: @gen81465
text:
I think the idea of this being "kid powered" (and I don't mean goat kids) is brilliant. There are too many children today becoming obese because they sit in front of the video game machines all day and never get out and get any exercise. Imagine a child that wants to play video games for 4 hours having to exercise to generate the power to run the machine and the tv. Video games might actually become a "healthy" activity.

I have a friend from back in high school whose daughter enjoys dogsled racing. The Alaskan Iditarod Race is a tribute to the working dogs who actually delivered diptheria medicine to Nome, Alaska in 1925 and helped save the town. A series of dogsled teams with drivers ran a course over 650 miles (over 1000 km) to accomplish what humans alone could not have done.

My godchild is now almost 4 years old and soon she will get her first electronic games, which will soon turn inta a struggle between her and her parents about how much time she can play on it. Most children like to play all day long and don't want to do any exercise anymore and I don't expect her to be different.
Now my idea is to make her some kind of generator (hand or pedal cranked) to charge a battery which powers the device. That way she can play as much as she likes, as long as she powers the device herselve.

replies.author: @markusgarvey

replies.author: @jb-dn3ct
replies.text: @Mark Estrada I've been having the same kinda thoughts, wood burners vent alot of waste heat straight outdoors, I used to build heat exchange units for oil rigs, basically a collection of brass pipes that carry water through them, submerged in oil heat is transferred to the water, heat exchangers are everywhere, a radiator is a heat exchanger as takes heat from hot water and exchanges it to the air, intercoolers on turbo charged cars are also heat exchangers, Roberts distillation coil is pretty much a heat exchanger, having said that it might be worth looking into as I don't know if removing to much heat might inadvertently cause it to condense the gases

author: @bartronicsecurity
text: Not many people know it but most modern exercise  bikes actually have a generator with an adjustable load which is set on a dial on the  control panel. Simply remove the control panel and get it to charge a 12 volt battery. It really is that simple. BTW expect around 10 to 20 amps at high load so make sure you use a large battery.  If the battery is too small then it will over charge from too high a charge current.

replies.author: @garyfinch1840
replies.text: You need to appreciate that although the amps are high the voltage is low so there really isn't much power there at all. The average human can output at most can 300watts/second. This needs to be maintained for an hour to generate just 0.3 kWh. A solar panel is a better solution by far.

replies.author: @bartronicsecurity
replies.text: @garyfinch1840 You are spot on. Maybe not quite with the 300watt for the average person.  That is a fit person.  I think the average is close to 200w per sec.  Yeah I do agree with you on the solar panels but why waste energy. If you had 10 people on bicycles in a gym for 1 hours each that is enough to feed the lights for the day for free. And you don't have to buy or fit solar panels.

replies.author: @garyfinch1840
replies.text: @bartronicsecurity yes I am probably above average. The idea only really applies for a gym and some do indeed utilise this to cover their lighting bill.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @scotttovey
text:
You can use a cat, but using the cat won't be as efficient as the dog the goat and the pig. You also won't get as much electricity as the size of the charging system would need to be small enough so that it did not discourage the cat from playing with it.

You would have to make a ball that had a small generator that charged a battery or a set of capacitors when the cat is playing with the ball.

Obviously, the cat won't play with the ball on command, so to get the cat motivated when you need a charge, make sure the ball has a small compartment where you can insert a bit of catnip.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
A very long time ago, when I was on my early teens, we used the dairy goats to carry our packs for 1-3 day hikes. They loved getting to go walk as a "herd" and sample a wider variety of forage, we could just wear light day-packs while they carried the bulk of tarps, cordage, water, food and, of course, milk. They would always get a few (it's not safe that they have more that a few bites) treats of grain or sweet-feed at the end of the day, we got to explore much more area than had we the full weight and there was fresh milk morning and evening.
I hang thought about this in a long time but I'm now recalling one who was very productive and how she's stop in the early afternoon and stubbornly refuse to take another step until she had a midday milking. LOL, those were some good days.
Thanks for stirring up that old memory! Makes me want to get a few goats.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is an awesome story - thanks for sharing mate

author: @crankfwd8209
text: The woman in the ground floor flat below me has a whole menagerie of animals including four medium sized dogs, couple of guinea pigs, and I'm sure I saw a chicken somewhere. It means I can turn my heating off in winter and keep warm from the upward heat.
